claim: Hybrid search combining BM25 keyword search with pgvector semantic search solves the core RAG failure mode: vector-only search incorrectly matches similar concepts (e.g., PostgreSQL 16 connection guide for a PostgreSQL 17 query)
actionable: Implement hybrid search using BM25 for exact term/version matching combined with pgvector for semantic similarity, ranking results by reciprocal rank fusion to prioritize items that score high on both
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Jacky Liang: Why Agents Can't Find the Right Docs (And How Postgres Fixes It)

claim: PostgreSQL 8.3+ includes built-in TS vector/TS query for keyword search with stemming and tokenization, enabling BM25-style ranking via TS rank with proper IDF weighting
actionable: Use PostgreSQL's CREATE EXTENSION for full-text search capabilities; implement BM25 ranking with pgext_search plugin for modern keyword relevance instead of relying on older TS rank
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Jacky Liang: Why Agents Can't Find the Right Docs (And How Postgres Fixes It)

claim: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) formula (1 / (k + rank)) with k=60 is a proven, 15-year-old algorithm for combining vector and keyword search results, prioritizing items scoring high on both methods
actionable: Implement RRF by fetching top-20 results from vector search and top-20 from keyword search, then combine scores using the RRF formula with k=60 constant to rank final results
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Jacky Liang: Why Agents Can't Find the Right Docs (And How Postgres Fixes It)

claim: Complex multi-system RAG architectures (PostgreSQL + Elasticsearch + vector DB) create maintenance nightmares: eventual consistency delays, schema synchronization failures, and cascading resyncs when systems fail
actionable: Consolidate your RAG stack into a single PostgreSQL instance with pgvector_scale and pg_text_search extensions rather than maintaining separate vector and keyword search systems
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Jacky Liang: Why Agents Can't Find the Right Docs (And How Postgres Fixes It)

claim: BM25 ranking penalizes keyword stuffing and term saturation (unlike TS rank), making it robust to artificially inflated keyword frequency in documents
actionable: When implementing keyword search for documentation RAG, use BM25-based ranking instead of raw term frequency to avoid gaming the system with repeated keywords
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Jacky Liang: Why Agents Can't Find the Right Docs (And How Postgres Fixes It)

claim: PostgreSQL's DISK_ANN (developed by Microsoft) provides sub-linear vector search performance comparable to specialized vector databases like Pinecone when using cosine similarity distance metric
actionable: When setting up pgvector_scale indexes in PostgreSQL, use DISK_ANN algorithm with cosine similarity to achieve production-grade vector search performance without separate infrastructure
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Jacky Liang: Why Agents Can't Find the Right Docs (And How Postgres Fixes It)

claim: LLMs perform poorly at selecting the 'right' context from a large set of search results; serve pre-filtered best results via hybrid retrieval before inference to prevent hallucinations and mismatches
actionable: Use hybrid search to deliver the top 5-10 most relevant documents to your LLM rather than trusting the LLM to filter from a larger candidate set, reducing hallucinations
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Jacky Liang: Why Agents Can't Find the Right Docs (And How Postgres Fixes It)

claim: Hybrid search is now available in PostgreSQL via Timescale's pg_text_search plugin (BM25) + pgvector_scale, eliminating the need for external search infrastructure like Elasticsearch or Pinecone
actionable: Install pg_text_search and pgvector_scale on Timescale PostgreSQL (free tier available) to implement production hybrid search without vendor lock-in to Elasticsearch or vector DB services
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Jacky Liang: Why Agents Can't Find the Right Docs (And How Postgres Fixes It)
